questions.question: What percentage of the Earth's volume is occupied by the 'Crust'?
questions.explanation: The crust forms only 1 per cent of the volume of the earth, while 84 per cent consists of the mantle and 15 per cent makes the core.

questions.question: The deepest mine in the world is located in South Africa. Approximately how deep is it?
questions.explanation: The deepest mine in South Africa is about 4 km deep. In search for oil, engineers have dug a hole about 6 km deep.

questions.question: To reach the center of the earth, which is impossible, how deep a hole would you have to dig on the ocean floor?
questions.explanation: The radius of the earth is approximately 6371 km; hence you would need to dig about 6000 km to reach the center.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the difference between 'Sial' and 'Sima'?

options.A: Sial is oceanic; Sima is continental
options.B: Sial is made of silica and alumina; Sima is silica and magnesium
options.C: Sial is the mantle; Sima is the core
options.D: Sial is liquid; Sima is solid

questions.explanation: Sial (Si-Silica, Al-Alumina) makes up the continental crust, while Sima (Si-Silica, Ma-Magnesium) makes up the oceanic crust.

questions.question: Why is the core of the earth under very high pressure and temperature?

options.A: Because it is made of gold
options.B: Due to the weight of the overlying layers (mantle and crust)
options.C: Because it is close to the sun

questions.explanation: The central core has very high temperature and pressure because it is buried under thousands of kilometers of rock layers.

questions.question: How are extrusive igneous rocks formed?

options.A: Magma cools slowly deep inside the earth
options.B: Lava comes on the surface and cools down rapidly
options.D: Rocks are melted by chemical reactions

questions.explanation: When molten lava comes on the earth’s surface, it rapidly cools down and becomes solid to form extrusive igneous rocks.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of 'Intrusive Igneous Rocks'?

options.A: They have a very fine-grained structure
options.B: They form on the surface of the earth
options.C: They form large grains because they cool down slowly

questions.explanation: Since they cool down slowly deep inside the earth, they form large grains. Granite is an example.

questions.question: The Deccan Plateau in India is primarily made up of which type of rock?
questions.explanation: The Deccan plateau is made up of basalt rocks, which are extrusive igneous rocks.

questions.question: Grinding stones used to prepare paste/powder of spices and grains are made of:
questions.explanation: Granite is a hard, intrusive igneous rock commonly used for making grinding stones.

questions.question: What happens when rocks roll down, crack, and hit each other?

options.B: They break into small fragments called sediments

questions.explanation: Physical weathering breaks rocks into smaller fragments known as sediments.

questions.question: Which type of rock is most likely to contain fossils?
questions.explanation: Sedimentary rocks are formed by layers of deposits which can trap the remains of plants and animals.

questions.question: Under great heat and pressure, Clay changes into ______.
questions.explanation: The textbook mentions that clay changes into slate and limestone into marble under metamorphic conditions.

questions.question: The 'Rock Cycle' starts with which type of rock melting into magma?
questions.explanation: Metamorphic rocks which are still under great heat and pressure melt down to form molten magma, but any rock can eventually be subducted and melted.

questions.question: Lava is actually ______ molten magma coming out from the interior of the earth.
questions.explanation: Lava is defined as fiery red molten magma that reaches the Earth's surface.

questions.question: The Latin word 'Ignis' means:
questions.explanation: Igneous is derived from the Latin word Ignis meaning fire.

questions.question: The Latin word 'Sedimentum' means:
questions.explanation: Sedimentary is derived from the Latin word sedimentum meaning settle down.

questions.question: The Greek word 'Metamorphose' means:
questions.explanation: Metamorphic is derived from the Greek word metamorphose meaning change of form.

questions.question: Which layer of the Earth has the highest temperature and pressure?
questions.explanation: The central core has extremely high temperature and pressure conditions.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a use of rocks mentioned in the chapter?
questions.explanation: Rocks are used for construction and games but not for making clothes.

questions.question: The crust is thinnest on the ______.
questions.explanation: The crust is only about 5 km thick on the ocean floors compared to 35 km on continental masses.

questions.question: What is the result of the cooling of molten magma in the rock cycle?
questions.explanation: In the rock cycle, molten magma cools and solidifies to become igneous rock.

questions.question: Limestone changes into which metamorphic rock?
questions.explanation: Under great heat and pressure, limestone (sedimentary) turns into marble (metamorphic).

questions.question: Which mineral is used in industries like aluminum and medicine?
questions.explanation: Minerals like bauxite (for aluminum), iron, and others are used in various industries.

questions.question: How do sediments turn into sedimentary rocks?

options.B: They are compressed and hardened to form layers

questions.explanation: Sediments are compressed and hardened over millions of years to form layers of sedimentary rock.

questions.question: The radius of the Earth is ______ km.
questions.explanation: The actual radius of the earth is 6,371 km as per the 'Do You Know' section of the chapter.
